purpose: Reduce package warpage during post-mold cure for AI processor package
new technology: Low CTE mold compound with thin-core substrate
DOE factor: Mold cure temp: 165/175/185 C; Mold cure time: 120/150/180 min; Substrate thickness: 180/220/260 um
DOE result: Strip warpage improved from 162 um to 58 um at room temperature
best pecipe setting: Mold cure 175 C x 150 min; substrate 220 um; clamp 60 s
engineer insight(lesson learned): Warpage was dominated by mold-compound and core-thickness interaction so material screening alone was not enough.

purpose: Optimize capillary underfill flow and fillet shape for flip-chip mobile application
new technology: Low-viscosity no-bleed underfill
DOE factor: Underfill dispense temp: 70/80/90 C; Needle height: 60/80/100 um; Substrate preheat: 90/110/130 C
DOE result: Underfill void rate decreased from 3.8% to 0.4%
best pecipe setting: Dispense temp 80 C; needle 80 um; preheat 110 C; speed 10 mm/s
engineer insight(lesson learned): Preheat and dispense temperature showed strong interaction because they jointly control viscosity at the flow front.

purpose: Reduce die attach voiding and improve thermal path in power management IC package
new technology: Silver sinter die attach for high-power density device
DOE factor: Sinter pressure: 8/12/16 MPa; Sinter temp: 220/240/260 C; Paste thickness: 20/30/40 um
DOE result: Junction-to-case thermal resistance improved from 1.82 C/W to 1.35 C/W
best pecipe setting: Pressure 12 MPa; sinter 240 C; paste 30 um; pre-dry 10 min
engineer insight(lesson learned): The best thermal result came from balancing bond-line thickness and void control rather than minimizing thickness alone.

purpose: Improve solder joint reliability after board-level temperature cycling
new technology: Cu pillar bump with NiPdAu finish
DOE factor: Reflow peak temp: 235/240/245 C; UBM thickness: 3/5/7 um; Cu pillar height: 22/26/30 um
DOE result: TCB first-failure cycle improved from 420 cycles to 760 cycles
best pecipe setting: Peak 240 C; soak 90 s; UBM 5 um; Cu pillar 26 um
engineer insight(lesson learned): Metallurgy stack and bump geometry interacted strongly so solder profile tuning alone could not close the reliability gap.

purpose: Improve micro-bump bonding yield for high-bandwidth memory integration
new technology: Thermo-compression bonding for fine-pitch micro-bump
DOE factor: Bond temp: 250/265/280 C; Bond force: 20/30/40 N; Flux amount: 0.6/0.8/1.0 mg
DOE result: Micro-bump non-open yield improved from 94.2% to 99.1%
best pecipe setting: Bond 265 C; force 30 N; hold 5 s; flux 0.8 mg
engineer insight(lesson learned): The process window was mainly limited by flux residue and alignment drift not by bonding temperature alone.

purpose: Reduce RDL crack and improve mechanical integrity after thermal cycling
new technology: Photosensitive polyimide with low-stress Cu plating stack
DOE factor: PI cure temp: 200/220/240 C; Cu plating thickness: 8/10/12 um; Panel debond temp: 120/140/160 C
DOE result: RDL crack occurrence reduced from 14/40 panels to 2/40 panels
best pecipe setting: PI cure 220 C; Cu 10 um; seed etch 60 s; debond 140 C
engineer insight(lesson learned): The lowest crack rate came from stress balancing across the dielectric and Cu stack rather than using the thickest metal option.

purpose: Lower head-in-pillow and non-wet opens for fine-pitch server package assembly
new technology: Vacuum reflow with low-oxidation solder paste
DOE factor: Peak temp: 240/245/250 C; Time above liquidus: 45/60/75 s; Vacuum level: 20/40/60 mbar
DOE result: NWO defect rate reduced from 1850 ppm to 220 ppm
best pecipe setting: Peak 245 C; TAL 60 s; vacuum 40 mbar; deposit 100%
engineer insight(lesson learned): Vacuum level improved wetting consistency but excessive TAL started to widen IMC and reduce margin.

purpose: Improve MSL3 and uHAST reliability for automotive grade package
new technology: High Tg epoxy mold compound with plasma-enhanced leadframe cleaning
DOE factor: Plasma power: 300/450/600 W; Plasma time: 30/45/60 s; Post mold cure: 175/180/185 C
DOE result: uHAST pass rate improved from 86.0% to 98.0%
best pecipe setting: Plasma 450 W x 45 s; gel 22 s; PMC 180 C
engineer insight(lesson learned): Surface preparation contributed more to uHAST performance than small changes in post-mold cure temperature.

purpose: Reduce package warpage during post-mold cure for AI processor package
new technology: Low CTE mold compound with thin-core substrate
DOE factor: Mold cure time: 120/150/180 min; Substrate thickness: 180/220/260 um; Post mold clamp time: 30/60/90 s
DOE result: Coplanarity pass rate improved from 91.8% to 98.6%
best pecipe setting: Mold cure 175 C x 180 min; substrate 220 um; clamp 90 s
engineer insight(lesson learned): The flattest profile came from mid-level cure conditions rather than the highest cure temperature.

purpose: Optimize capillary underfill flow and fillet shape for flip-chip mobile application
new technology: Low-viscosity no-bleed underfill
DOE factor: Needle height: 60/80/100 um; Substrate preheat: 90/110/130 C; Dispense speed: 8/10/12 mm/s
DOE result: Flow completion time reduced from 21.5 s to 14.2 s
best pecipe setting: Dispense temp 90 C; needle 60 um; preheat 110 C; speed 8 mm/s
engineer insight(lesson learned): Fast flow alone was not the right target and stable fillet geometry was the better predictor for reliability.

purpose: Reduce die attach voiding and improve thermal path in power management IC package
new technology: Silver sinter die attach for high-power density device
DOE factor: Sinter pressure: 8/12/16 MPa; Paste thickness: 20/30/40 um; Pre-dry time: 5/10/15 min
DOE result: Die attach void rate decreased from 7.2% to 0.9%
best pecipe setting: Pressure 16 MPa; sinter 240 C; paste 20 um; pre-dry 10 min
engineer insight(lesson learned): Void reduction depended more on pressure uniformity than on simply increasing peak temperature.

purpose: Improve solder joint reliability after board-level temperature cycling
new technology: Cu pillar bump with NiPdAu finish
DOE factor: Soak time: 60/90/120 s; UBM thickness: 3/5/7 um; Cu pillar height: 22/26/30 um
DOE result: Daisy-chain open rate after temp cycle reduced from 12.5% to 2.1%
best pecipe setting: Peak 240 C; soak 120 s; UBM 7 um; Cu pillar 26 um
engineer insight(lesson learned): Cross-section review showed crack initiation near the UBM corner so the next DOE should include pad design factors.

purpose: Improve micro-bump bonding yield for high-bandwidth memory integration
new technology: Thermo-compression bonding for fine-pitch micro-bump
DOE factor: Bond force: 20/30/40 N; Bond hold time: 3/5/7 s; Flux amount: 0.6/0.8/1.0 mg
DOE result: Contact resistance improved from 21.4 mOhm to 12.6 mOhm
best pecipe setting: Bond 265 C; force 40 N; hold 5 s; flux 0.6 mg
engineer insight(lesson learned): Bond force helped collapse variation but too much force increased edge deformation risk on outer-row bumps.

purpose: Reduce RDL crack and improve mechanical integrity after thermal cycling
new technology: Photosensitive polyimide with low-stress Cu plating stack
DOE factor: PI cure temp: 200/220/240 C; Seed etch time: 45/60/75 s; Panel debond temp: 120/140/160 C
DOE result: Temp-cycle pass rate improved from 88.5% to 97.9%
best pecipe setting: PI cure 240 C; Cu 10 um; seed etch 45 s; debond 140 C
engineer insight(lesson learned): Debond temperature was a hidden sensitivity because it changed residual stress before the next build-up layer.

purpose: Lower head-in-pillow and non-wet opens for fine-pitch server package assembly
new technology: Vacuum reflow with low-oxidation solder paste
DOE factor: Time above liquidus: 45/60/75 s; Vacuum level: 20/40/60 mbar; Paste deposit volume: 85/100/115 %
DOE result: Head-in-pillow occurrence dropped from 1.9% to 0.2%
best pecipe setting: Peak 250 C; TAL 45 s; vacuum 40 mbar; deposit 100%
engineer insight(lesson learned): The best assembly window balanced NWO reduction with IMC growth control which is more realistic for product transfer.

purpose: Improve MSL3 and uHAST reliability for automotive grade package
new technology: High Tg epoxy mold compound with plasma-enhanced leadframe cleaning
DOE factor: Plasma power: 300/450/600 W; Mold compound gel time: 18/22/26 s; Post mold cure: 175/180/185 C
DOE result: Package delamination area dropped from 11.2% to 1.7%
best pecipe setting: Plasma 600 W x 30 s; gel 22 s; PMC 180 C
engineer insight(lesson learned): The cleanest interface was achieved at moderate plasma power which avoided over-etch damage on the leadframe surface.

purpose: Reduce package warpage during post-mold cure for AI processor package
new technology: Low CTE mold compound with thin-core substrate
DOE factor: Mold cure temp: 165/175/185 C; Substrate thickness: 180/220/260 um; Post mold clamp time: 30/60/90 s
DOE result: Shadow moire peak warpage reduced from 148 um to 52 um after cure
best pecipe setting: Mold cure 185 C x 120 min; substrate 260 um; clamp 60 s
engineer insight(lesson learned): Substrate thickness needs to be blocked in follow-up DOE because it shifts the entire warpage baseline.

purpose: Optimize capillary underfill flow and fillet shape for flip-chip mobile application
new technology: Low-viscosity no-bleed underfill
DOE factor: Underfill dispense temp: 70/80/90 C; Substrate preheat: 90/110/130 C; Dispense speed: 8/10/12 mm/s
DOE result: Fillet height Cpk improved from 1.02 to 1.68
best pecipe setting: Dispense temp 80 C; needle 80 um; preheat 130 C; speed 10 mm/s
engineer insight(lesson learned): A narrower needle improved edge control but made the process more sensitive to dispense gap variation.

purpose: Reduce die attach voiding and improve thermal path in power management IC package
new technology: Silver sinter die attach for high-power density device
DOE factor: Sinter temp: 220/240/260 C; Paste thickness: 20/30/40 um; Pre-dry time: 5/10/15 min
DOE result: Die shear strength increased from 28 kgf to 41 kgf
best pecipe setting: Pressure 12 MPa; sinter 260 C; paste 30 um; pre-dry 5 min
engineer insight(lesson learned): Silver sinter showed good RnD promise but tool parallelism control is still a scale-up risk for pilot line transfer.

purpose: Improve solder joint reliability after board-level temperature cycling
new technology: Cu pillar bump with NiPdAu finish
DOE factor: Reflow peak temp: 235/240/245 C; Soak time: 60/90/120 s; Cu pillar height: 22/26/30 um
DOE result: Cross-section crack occurrence dropped from 9/30 units to 1/30 units
best pecipe setting: Peak 245 C; soak 60 s; UBM 5 um; Cu pillar 30 um
engineer insight(lesson learned): The mid-height Cu pillar gave the best fatigue life because it balanced compliance and wetting stability.
